steps.title: Fastest path: identify the scale factor first
steps.content: Count the relevant dimensions or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Use the area scale factor for similar figures
steps.content: When linear dimensions scale by a factor of $k$, areas scale by $k^2$. Since the side of square M is 8 times the side of square N, the area of square M is $8^2 = 64$ times the area of square N.

steps.title: Calculate the area of square M
steps.content:
Since the area of square N is 36 square centimeters:
$$\text{Area}_M = 64 \times 36 = 2,304$$
A is correct.
